attributes.description: Alerts when `sqlserver.processes.blocked > 0`. Any active blocking chain degrades throughput for multiple sessions.
attributes.name: [SQL Server OTel] Blocked processes

investigation_guide.blob:
## SQL Server blocked processes

### What fired
`sqlserver.processes.blocked > 0` during the window.

### Why it matters
Blocked processes are queries waiting on locks held by others. Long or frequent blocking cascades into thread exhaustion and timeouts.

### Triage
1. Use the Concurrency dashboard to see blocking chains.
2. Check `sqlserver.lock.wait.rate` for corroboration.

### Remediation
- Kill the head blocker if appropriate.
- Optimise transactions to reduce lock hold time.

### Tuning
- Fires on any non-zero value.

esqlQuery.esql:
FROM metrics-sqlserverreceiver.otel-default
// Non-zero blocked processes means active blocking chains
| WHERE metrics.sqlserver.processes.blocked IS NOT NULL
| STATS max_blocked = MAX(metrics.sqlserver.processes.blocked),
       instance_id = MAX(resource.attributes.service.instance.id)
  BY COALESCE(resource.attributes.service.instance.id, host.name, "unknown")
| WHERE max_blocked > 0
| KEEP instance_id, max_blocked
